media.name: Salt minimal medium (MM) with ibuprofen (IBU) as sole carbon source (PMC11892951)
media.base_medium: Salt minimal medium (MM) of the Santero/Seville lab; a phosphate-buffered mineral salts medium derived from the mineral medium of Dorn et al. (1974)
media.base_medium_reference: In-text: 'salt minimal medium (MM) [43]'. Ref 43 = Hernaez MJ, Reineke W, Santero E. Genetic analysis of biodegradation of tetralin by a Sphingomonas strain. Appl Environ Microbiol. 1999;65(4):1806-1810. doi:10.1128/AEM.65.4.1806-1810.1999 (PMID 10103288; PMC91258). That paper in turn defines MM as the carbon-free mineral medium of Dorn E, Hellwig M, Reineke W, Knackmuss HJ. Isolation and characterization of a 3-chlorobenzoate degrading pseudomonad. Arch Microbiol. 1974;99:61-70. doi:10.1007/BF00696222. Numeric salt composition below is taken from a documented reproduction of the Dorn (1974) base medium: Mehboob F et al., Appl Environ Microbiol 2009;75(23):7567-7574, PMC2690828.
media.verification: recipe recovered (base identity confirmed; some concentrations uncertain — see notes)
media.verification_evidence: Cultures in salt minimal medium (MM) [43] with IBU 1 g/L as carbon source were diluted one-fourth of the previous 20 ml saturated culture in aeration at 30°C for ~15 days ... Commercial IBU sodium salt was purchased from Sigma-Aldrich.
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC11892951 (see GrowthDB record).
